title: The Chronicle attributed to Robert of Gloucester (long recension)
summary: The checked-in CME00022 XML represents a University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse encoding keyed from Samuel Bagster's 1810 reset reprint of Thomas Hearne's 1724 two-volume edition, despite reproducing the 1724 title page. Hearne transcribed his main text from British Library Harley MS 201, appended the continuation from British Library Cotton MS Caligula A XI where Harley ends, and selectively collated College of Arms Arundel MS 58 for readings marked Ar. The encoded selection is the old title page and Chronicle pp. 1–571; it omits the 1810 series title, Hearne's publisher's preface and contents, and the appendix, glossary, index, and other back matter. Complete public scans of both 1810 volumes and both 1724 volumes are cached with scan maps, as is the principal research thesis. No complete public facsimile of any of the three manuscripts was verified on 2026-07-12; current catalog and mediated consultation routes are recorded instead.
review_notes: The abstract cataloging subject is the Middle English long recension; the witnesses, Hearne's hybrid edition, Bagster's reset reprint, and the digital encodings are separate lineage layers.
The Mercier and Chervet colophon at printed p. 364 occurs in the 1810 scan and encoded XML but not at p. 364 of the 1724 first edition, confirming the immediate reset-reprint source.
Generic U-M collection production statements were not transferred to this item. The XML documents a 2016-09-23 review, not the initial keying date or method.
Complete facsimile coverage means the provider's complete exposed physical scan object; the work-bearing portions are recorded separately in local_copies[].work_portion.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00022
notes: The file is 1,231,772 bytes and is pinned through the recorded CME submodule commit.
Its TEMPHEAD records a 2016-09-23 review that added TEMPHEAD, proofed the title page, checked tags and structure, and corrected page milestones; this is not an initial creation statement.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME digital text CME00022

date_statements.label: documented XML review
date_statements.notes: The TEMPHEAD revision documents review and correction activity, not the initial transcription date.

notes: The encoding includes the reproduced 1724 title page and Chronicle pp. 1–571 from the 1810 reset, with Hearne's notes embedded in the text.
It excludes the 1810 Works series title, publisher's preface, contents, volume half-title, appendix beginning p. 572, glossary, index, and other back matter.
It has 94 LG and 12,198 L elements; one L is nested inside a NOTE, leaving 12,197 direct work-text lines.

entities.label: Samuel Bagster's 1810 reset reprint of Hearne's Robert of Gloucester's Chronicle
alternate_labels: The Works of Thomas Hearne, vols. I–II

bibliographic.citation: Thomas Hearne, ed., Robert of Gloucester's Chronicle, in The Works of Thomas Hearne, vols. I–II (London: printed for Samuel Bagster, 1810).
bibliographic.title: Robert of Gloucester's Chronicle
bibliographic.series: The Works of Thomas Hearne, vols. I–II
bibliographic.pages: Chronicle pp. 1–571; subsequent apparatus and indexes through p. 792

notes: The series title reads The Works of Thomas Hearne, printed for Samuel Bagster in the Strand, 1810, while the following old title page reproduces the 1724 Oxford wording.

The text is newly typeset rather than a photographic facsimile. Printed p. 364 ends with 'Printed by Mercier and Chervet, No. 32, Little Bartholomew Close, London,' a diagnostic present in the XML and absent from the 1724 p. 364.
Volume I contains the prefatory material and Chronicle pp. 1–364. Volume II resumes at p. 367 after a half-title and contains the Chronicle through p. 571; appendices begin on p. 572.

entities.label: Thomas Hearne's 1724 edition of Robert of Gloucester's Chronicle
alternate_labels: Robert of Gloucester's Chronicle, Transcrib'd, and now first publish'd, from a MS. in the Harleyan Library

bibliographic.citation: Thomas Hearne, ed., Robert of Gloucester's Chronicle, Transcrib'd, and now first publish'd, from a MS. in the Harleyan Library, 2 vols. (Oxford: printed at the Theater, 1724).
bibliographic.title: Robert of Gloucester's Chronicle
bibliographic.pages: Chronicle pp. 1–571, with preface, appendix, glossary, index, and related matter

notes: Hearne says he abandoned a Bodleian copy, transcribed the D'Ewes/Harley manuscript supplied through Richard Mead as his base text, and appended the entire Cotton continuation where Harley ends.
He compared the substantially altered College of Arms manuscript supplied through John Bridges, omitted most of its prose and interpolated material, and marked selected observations and readings Ar.
Hearne's statements about author, manuscript age, completeness, and intended ending are historical editorial claims, not adopted uncritically as modern work facts.

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 201

date_statements.label: production
date_statements.value: first quarter of the fifteenth century (1400–1424)

holding.notes: The Robert of Gloucester Chronicle occupies ff. 4r–161r.

physical_description.extent: i + 162 folios, with an unfoliated front paper flyleaf and two rear paper flyleaves
physical_description.format: codex; Middle English rhyming chronicle in Gothic script
notes: Blank parchment precedes f. 4; f. 31 is misbound and should precede f. 30.
The manuscript was rebound by the British Museum in 1951 and was produced in England.
The British Library explicitly records its use by Hearne and notes that it supplies text missing in Cotton Caligula A XI.
The physical manuscript extends beyond the work component only minimally, but the complete codex—not just work folios—would be the correct facsimile target.

entities.label: British Library Cotton MS Caligula A XI

date_statements.label: production
date_statements.value: last quarter of the thirteenth century (1275–1300)
date_statements.notes: This is the British Library's current component date. It is recorded separately from Hearne's historical opinion and the internally argued post-1297 or post-1307 date of the long recension.

holding.notes: The Chronicle occupies ff. 3r–13v and 15r–168v; the component record is labeled ff. 3–13, 15–169.

physical_description.support: principally parchment, with paper at ff. 1, 75, and 81
physical_description.extent: 288 folios in the whole composite codex
notes: The manuscript was damaged in the 1731 Cotton Library fire and rebound by the British Museum in 1951.
The Robert of Gloucester component is cataloged as the first version and imperfect.
The codex also contains the Short Metrical Chronicle at f. 14, Piers Plowman B at ff. 170–286, and De vita monachorum at ff. 287–288; these adjacent works are not part of CME00022.
Hearne's printed continuation head cites Cotton Library, Caligula A. XI, f. 130b at the point where Harley ends.

entities.label: College of Arms Arundel MS 58

date_statements.label: production
date_statements.value: fifteenth century; Fisher describes a mid-fifteenth-century parchment codex

holding.notes: Consultation is by appointment in the College of Arms record room.

notes: Fisher's codicological discussion identifies three scribal portions and analyzes the manuscript's substantial prose remodeling and interpolations.

The AIM25 description calls the text a History of England that remodels Robert of Gloucester's metrical chronicle with interpolations and additions from the Brut, Geoffrey of Monmouth, William of Malmesbury, and other sources, continuing to 1332.
It is a substantively remodeled derivative witness and comparison source, not Hearne's base manuscript and not a simple upstream copy of every encoded line.

entities.label: Internet Archive / Wellcome complete scans of Hearne's Works, 1810, volumes I–II
entities.reproduction_format: two complete PDF scan objects with XML scan maps
notes: The PDF files have 470 and 452 pages; Internet Archive metadata reports 472 and 454 source images, including two excluded color cards per volume.
The cached complete objects preserve covers, endleaves, prefatory matter, adjacent apparatus, and the full two-volume publication, not only CME00022's encoded pages.

The scans reproduce the two complete published volumes rather than manuscript objects.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Multiple manuscript copies with different extents and textual forms
editorial_practices.representation: Harley MS 201 supplies the main text and Cotton MS Caligula A XI supplies the entire continuation where Harley stops.
editorial_practices.description: Hearne chose the D'Ewes/Harley manuscript as his base after abandoning a Bodleian copy, then spliced in the Cotton continuation beginning at printed p. 465. He treated the two sources as complementary portions of one printed Chronicle.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Arundel MS 58 substantially remodels the Chronicle, changing language, rearranging, omitting and inserting text, and mixing prose with verse.
editorial_practices.representation: Selected observations and readings are entered in the apparatus and marked Ar.; most remodeled material is omitted.
editorial_practices.description: Hearne compared the College of Arms copy but did not treat it as a coequal base text. This selective use matters because its readings can enter the printed apparatus without making the whole Arundel remodeling upstream of CME00022.
editorial_practices.scope: Selected apparatus notes throughout the 1724 edition

editorial_practices.source_feature: Hearne's 1724 two-volume edition, including its old title page, preface, Chronicle, apparatus, and indexes
editorial_practices.representation: A reset reprint in modernized type with a new Works series title, London publisher statement, and Mercier and Chervet printer colophon, while retaining the old 1724 title page.
editorial_practices.description: The 1810 volumes reproduce Hearne's edition as a new typographic version. Their retained old title page can conceal the reset layer unless series-title and colophon evidence are inspected.
editorial_practices.scope: The Works of Thomas Hearne, vols. I–II

editorial_practices.source_feature: The 1810 two-volume publication contains series title pages, Hearne's preface and contents, Chronicle pp. 1–571, appendix, glossary, index, and other matter.
editorial_practices.representation: The XML retains the reproduced 1724 title page, Chronicle pp. 1–571, and embedded Hearne notes while omitting the 1810 series title, prefatory prose, volume half-title, and back matter after p. 571.
editorial_practices.description: The electronic selection creates a coherent encoded Chronicle but does not reproduce the entire source edition. Its supplement DIV2 explicitly marks the Cotton continuation beginning at printed p. 465.

evidence.citation: Thomas Hearne, ed., Robert of Gloucester's Chronicle, in The Works of Thomas Hearne, vols. I–II (London: printed for Samuel Bagster, 1810), Wellcome / Internet Archive complete scans.
evidence.locator: vol. I PDF pp. 11–13, 64–78, 99–462; vol. II PDF pp. 11–15, 15–220; Chronicle pp. 1–572
evidence.summary: Primary immediate-edition evidence for the 1810 Works series title, reproduced 1724 old title, Hearne's witness-selection and collation statements, complete Chronicle pages, p. 364 printer colophon, Cotton continuation head at p. 465, ending at p. 571, and appendix boundary at p. 572. Both complete PDFs were cached and inspected.

evidence.citation: Thomas Hearne, publisher's preface to Robert of Gloucester's Chronicle (1724; reset 1810), pp. lii–lxvi.
evidence.locator: 1810 vol. I PDF pp. 64–65 and 77–78; corresponding 1724 vol. I PDF pp. 52–54 and 64–68
evidence.summary: Hearne's direct editorial account: he transcribed the D'Ewes/Harley copy as his chosen text, compared the College of Arms copy and used selected readings marked Ar., and appended the Cotton copy's entire continuation where Harley ends. His physical and chronological opinions are kept as attributed historical claims.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive items b33291020_0001 and b33291020_0002, The Works of Thomas Hearne, Wellcome Library scans.
evidence.locator: item metadata, file inventories, image counts, rights fields, and scan maps
evidence.summary: Surrogate evidence for 1810 date and imprint, Wellcome provenance, volume numbers, complete 472- and 454-image scan sequences, PDF and scandata files, direct download URLs, and Public Domain Mark metadata.

evidence.citation: Thomas Hearne, ed., Robert of Gloucester's Chronicle, 2 vols. (Oxford: printed at the Theater, 1724), ECCO / Internet Archive complete scans.
evidence.locator: vol. I title and preface; Chronicle pp. 1–364; vol. II Chronicle pp. 367–571
evidence.summary: First-edition evidence for title, Oxford imprint, Hearne's source statements, original typography and page sequence, Cotton continuation, and edition scope. Direct comparison of p. 364 and sample pages establishes that the XML follows the 1810 reset rather than the 1724 typesetting.

evidence.citation: AIM25, College of Arms, Arundel Manuscripts, description of Arundel MS 58; College of Arms, Records and Collections.
evidence.locator: Arundel MS 58 description and Arundel collection overview
evidence.summary: Repository-level evidence that Arundel MS 58 is a fifteenth-century History of England remodeling Robert of Gloucester's metrical chronicle with prose and interpolations from other historical sources, continuing to 1332; the official College page identifies the Arundel collection's medieval historical focus.

evidence.citation: College of Arms, Archive and Conservation Departments.
evidence.locator: student room access and appointment conditions
evidence.summary: Official evidence that the College of Arms provides a small student room by advance appointment with the archivist and asks new researchers for a letter of introduction and photographic identification.

evidence.citation: Matthew Fisher, Once Called Albion: The Composition and Transmission of History Writing in England, 1280–1350 (DPhil thesis, University of Oxford, 2005), chapters 3–4.
evidence.summary: Specialist evidence for the uncertain Robert attribution, fifteen-manuscript and two-recension model, post-1297 and possible post-1307 long-recension dating, dialect localization, incomplete ending, witness sigla, and Arundel MS 58's codicology and extensive remodeling. The complete 5,892,257-byte thesis PDF was cached.

evidence.citation: Oxford University Research Archive, Once Called Albion, object 5b5c77fa-2308-4eda-936a-a39478de1b66.
evidence.locator: bibliographic metadata, date, author, rights holder, and downloadable file
evidence.summary: Institutional repository evidence for Fisher's authorship, DPhil status, 2005 thesis date, complete public PDF download, copyright attribution, and repository access context.

evidence.citation: Dated complete-facsimile search for British Library Harley MS 201, British Library Cotton MS Caligula A XI, and College of Arms Arundel MS 58.
evidence.locator: exact shelfmark and title searches; BL parent/component records and digitized-content filters; College/AIM25 collection routes
evidence.summary: On 2026-07-12 no complete public manuscript facsimile or public IIIF Presentation object was verified for any of the three witnesses. The BL records' Digitised Content fields were empty, and no College of Arms item-level image object surfaced. These are dated qualified negatives, not proof that no repository-held photography exists.
notes: No manuscript image was cached. A public scholarly transcription of Cotton encountered during searching is not a manuscript facsimile and was excluded from the image-access claim.

open_questions.question: Can complete, reusable facsimiles be obtained for Harley MS 201, Cotton MS Caligula A XI, and College of Arms Arundel MS 58?
next_steps: Ask the British Library whether complete existing photography, legacy microfilm, or unindexed digital objects exist for Harley 201 and Cotton Caligula A XI.
Ask the College of Arms whether complete photography of Arundel 58 exists and obtain written cost, retention, publication, and reuse terms.

If new photography is required, request every physical surface of each complete codex—bindings, boards, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, every leaf, and adjacent works—not only the Chronicle components.
After obtaining complete objects, map CME00022's exact work folios and source-splice passages separately with image/canvas locators and first/end deep links.
notes: No manuscript image entered the cache because no complete public deliverable was verified.

open_questions.question: Who composed and revised the two recensions, and how should the long recension's internally argued post-1297 or post-1307 date be reconciled with Cotton Caligula A XI's current 1275–1300 catalog date?
next_steps: Review post-2005 work on the Robert self-reference, recension chronology, and manuscript stemma.
Seek the British Library's current codicological basis for the Cotton component date.
Retain separate, explicitly attributed claims rather than forcing one normalized date.

open_questions.question: What source copy, initial keying method and date, correction history, and downloadable-source terms apply to the U-M CME00022 encoding?
next_steps: Ask U-M Digital Collections for item-level production metadata, source-copy provenance, and any source XML package.
Compare representative XML spellings, notes, and page breaks against the cached 1810 scan while preserving the already confirmed reset-edition boundary.
Keep the 2016-09-23 TEMPHEAD entry scoped to review rather than initial encoding.

open_questions.question: How much of the long recension's ending is lost, and did any medieval witness preserve the intended continuation through 1272 that Hearne believed had existed?
next_steps: Compare the endings of every long-recension witness and modern critical editions.
Inspect Cotton's terminal collation and fire damage directly or in a complete facsimile.
Distinguish Hearne's historical conjecture from recoverable witness evidence and from Fisher's statement that no transmitted complete ending survives.
